mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware optimizations and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM easily.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.1

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.19.1.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install vLLM with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM chart into the specified namespace, setting up the necessary resources for LLM serving.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.1

```

> Installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create a vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) for vLLM, which defines the model and serving parameters.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: my-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  parameters:
    maxTokens: 100
EOF

```

> Replace 'gpt-3' with your desired model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: my-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  parameters:
    maxTokens: 100
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> You should see 'my-vllm' in the list with a status of 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the vLLM Custom Resource.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This process includes draining the nodes if necessary, upgrading the Helm chart, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.1

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Backup the current vLLM configuration.
> Upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM resource is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Custom Resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the vLLM controller with 'kubectl logs -l app=vllm -n vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check your network settings.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits or add more nodes to the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pods may be in a crash loop or failing to start.

**Fix:**
Check the pod events with 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n vllm' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your Custom Resource definitions to match the new model parameters.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm' showing 'my-vllm' with a status of 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
